title: Cooling cycle servicing - refrigerant recovery/vacuuming/leak testing/refrigerant sealing

blocks.text: Cooling cycle servicing - refrigerant recovery/vacuuming/leak testing/refrigerant sealing
1. Manifold Gauge Set/Freon Gas Recovery/Reclaimer Installation
Install the manifold gauge set/CFC gas recovery/recycling unit.

items: Manifold gauge sets are, HFC-134a(R-134a) Use dedicated
CFC gas recovery/recycling equipment, HFC-134a(R-134a) Use only for the purpose for which it was designed, and use it correctly according to the instruction manual.

items.text: Manifold gauge sets are, HFC-134a(R-134a) Use dedicated
Manifold gauge sets are, HFC-134a(R-134a) Use dedicated
CFC gas recovery/recycling equipment, HFC-134a(R-134a) Use only for the purpose for which it was designed, and use it correctly according to the instruction manual.
CFC gas recovery/recycling equipment, HFC-134a(R-134a) Use only for the purpose for which it was designed, and use it correctly according to the instruction manual.

blocks.text: Start the engine. Turn on the A/C and run it for about 5 minutes (to recover as much compressor oil as possible from circulating in the A/C system). (to recover as much compressor oil circulating in the A/C system as possible to the compressor) Stop the A/C and then stop the engine. Recover refrigerant gas using a CFC gas recovery and regeneration unit.

Install the vacuum pump. Activate the vacuum pump and continue pulling vacuum for 15 minutes after the scale on the low pressure gauge reaches -0.09 MPa (-680 mmHg). Close each valve on the gauge and let stand for 5 minutes to confirm that there is no change in the needle of the gauge. If the gauge needle changes, use a leak tester to locate and repair the refrigerant leak.

items: Vacuum pumps should be equipped with a stop valve to prevent backflow of pump oil.

blocks.text: Leak testers should be inspected using an HFC-134a (R-134a) compatible tester.

Start the engine. Set the temperature control to MAX COOL and the inside/outside air switch to inside air circulation ( ), the mode position is set to VENT ( ), set the A/C switch to ON and fan speed to maximum. Open the front door fully. Refrigerant HFC-134a (R-134a) is sealed. Regulated refrigerant fill volume: 320 +25 -25 g Stop the engine. Inspect for gas leaks using a leak tester HFC-134a (R-134a) vs.

items: Engine speed should not exceed 1,500 rpm.
